summary: I read both design guides, then reviewed the full Dashboard UI diff against them — the `Dashboard::` query-object POROs, the `Dashboard::Broadcast` fan-out service and its wiring into `Complete`/`Sweep`/`Approve`/`BroadcastCard`/`Column`, the `HomeController`/`PhasesController` actions, all `home/*` partials, the `status`/`define` helpers, and the three Stimulus controllers. The architecture is strongly guide-aligned: business logic sits in reusable POROs, controllers stay thin and branch only on `result.success?`/response format, broadcasts fire from services after commit and target the smallest DOM unit over a per-user `:dashboard` stream, `status_badge` is the single semantic-color source with the status word always shown, the presentation-boundary `safely` rescue is scoped correctly, and Minitest covers queries/service/controllers/system flows — plus both genuinely new patterns were added to the guides in the same PR as CLAUDE.md requires. Since only `input.json` changed since iteration 6, I re-verified each item against the current tree rather than assuming: all six previously-noted deviations still stand (off-scale red/green status shades, a hand-rolled attention badge bypassing `status_badge`, run-selection/JSON-parse business logic living in a view helper, three empty states missing the mandated icon + action, the fleet panel polling instead of broadcasting without a sanctioning guide amendment, and off-scale spacing values). All are minor, guide-cited, and trivially fixable with no blockers or majors, so I emitted a `needs_work` verdict listing the six findings to `verdict.json`.
